methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Cellcolabs is a Series A-stage biotech company led by Mattias Bernow that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including peers such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $25M raised proxy, yielding $50M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (range: $100M–$3.3B) produces a median valuation of $805M. A public-comp anchoring approach using a 6.6x P/S median across three public biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) was not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $926M, with a median of $428M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage biotech valuation: peer multiples vary by three orders of magnitude depending on technology maturity, clinical progress, and market perception. The stage-progression method offers a floor tied directly to capital deployed, while the peer median reflects market practice for companies at series inflection points. Resolution of this range will depend on clarity around Cellcolabs' technical differentiation, regulatory pathway, and capital efficiency relative to the identified peer set.
